Added scope-guard note to swarm-wave SKILL.md workflow 1 (Plan a swarm sprint), placed as a Note immediately after step 7 and before workflow 2. Cites bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter (gastownhall/beads@5d524cf7:docs/INTEGRATION_CHARTER.md) as design rationale: cross-tracker orchestration (bd github sync) runs post-wave/post-sprint, not via swarm-wave. npm run check passes (17/17 tests, plugin/md/sh/hooks all green).

Created skills/synergy-tracker/references/synergy-bm-format.md mirroring upstream-tracker's basic-memory-friction-format.md: target type routing (sibling project notes via bm-entity field with projects/ fallback), target section structure (## Cross-Project Synergy with four subsections + Resolved), generalization transforms (strip paths/dates/local-triage; keep Status/Convergence path/Readiness/Priority/Effort enums), and edit_note gotchas (append+section EOF bug, find_replace anchoring, expected_replacements=1, read_note before edit). npm run check passes.

Added Workflow 4 (Trend Review) to synergy-tracker SKILL.md mirroring upstream-tracker structure: input signals (glob, count, age), processing steps (drift audit, reciprocation check, status sweep, BM cross-reference, dormancy scaling), Trend Review entry format, and integration with /retrospective. Updated Sprint Workflow Integration to reflect that only workflow 5 remains planned.

Added domain-fit pass/fail test to 'They Have / We Don't' section guidance in both SKILL.md and references/synergy-entry-format.md. Cited Sprint 19 worked failures (swarm-wave, vendor-sync) as evidence of category errors the test catches.

Added bilateral reciprocation mandate paragraph to workflow 1 (Log) after the Structured fields subsection. Includes verbatim BM quote and Sprint 19 evidence (3 actively drifting artifacts plus 1 stale aligned row surfaced by reciprocation pass). Mandates prompting user to file reciprocal entry on sibling project.

Added single-paragraph Charter citation to Guidelines section explaining bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter no-cross-tracker-orchestration rule as design rationale for synergy-tracker living in BM rather than bd.
bd find-duplicates step inserted in workflow 1; threshold + AI cost caveat in heuristics
vocabulary adoption complete: skill (Agent 2A), CLAUDE.md table (Agent 2B), MEMORY.md core types (Agent 2B)
Charter ref added to MEMORY.md + 2 [architecture] observations on brew-beads ### Tracker Integrations

user applied manual ~/.claude/settings.json edit removing redundant SessionStart bd prime block. Full verification (count of '# Beads Workflow Context' blocks = 1, not 2) requires a fresh session — visual confirmation deferred to next session bootstrap

registry schema extended with optional local-path field and .local.json companion override pattern; backward compatible; documented in both CLAUDE.md convention sections; symmetric across synergy-registry and vendor-registry

new /sibling-sync skill created with 4 workflows: discover, sync SYNERGY, sync UPSTREAM, apply reciprocation batch (opt-in). Read-only by default with --auto-reciprocate opt-in path. Cross-skill boundaries documented. CLAUDE.md updated with skill summary and sprint workflow cycle integration.

resolved via npm update — bumped picomatch 2.3.1 → 2.3.2 transitively (via chokidar via unified-args via remark-cli). Plus 7 other transitive deps updated. npm audit reports 0 vulnerabilities. npm run check passes.

Smoke test complete: all 4 scenarios passed structurally against ../vp-claude. Filed 3 refinement bugs (tl8 P2, 2ad P3, wuc P3); skill is release-ready as-is — bugs are spec-refinement, not behavior blockers.

Fixed pre-release in 0.12.0 sibling-sync refinement: 'They Have / We Don't' explicitly excluded from findings (a)/(b) in workflow 2 step 3, restated in findings (a)/(b) bullets, and added as a hard-limit guard in workflow 4. Defense-in-depth across 3 sites.

Fixed pre-release in 0.12.0 sibling-sync refinement: title-keyed comparison restructured to explicit 2-pass form — Pass 1 deterministic lead-clause match (lowercase, whitespace-collapse, truncate at first :, —, --, or '('), Pass 2 judgment on residuals only with explicit prefix-collision guard. Workflow 4 uses Pass 1 only with Pass 2 as advisory disambiguation prompts (cost asymmetry inverts at mutation boundary).

Fixed pre-release in 0.12.0 sibling-sync refinement: finding (d) renamed from 'Divergence convergence-status drift' to 'Status drift' and broadened to cover both Shared Patterns (Status field disagreement: aligned/drifting/diverging) and Divergences with adopt-theirs/propose-shared. Excludes Divergences with accept-difference (intended-asymmetric).

Workflow 3 now detects two pairing modes: Mode A (basename intersection, current behavior) and Mode B (reciprocal sibling-friction pairs UPSTREAM-<sibling>.md ↔ UPSTREAM-<this>.md). Added findings (e)/(f)/(g)/(h) for Mode B; pinned 'shipped' definition; canonical project-name derivation from .claude-plugin/plugin.json. CLAUDE.md skills entry updated. npm run check passes (17/17 hook tests + plugin validation + remark + shellcheck). Read-only contract preserved; workflow 4 hard limits unchanged.

Shipped in v0.12.1: new shared reference doc skills/synergy-tracker/references/project-name-derivation.md with four-tier precedence (sibling-registry back-pointer → plugin manifest → package manifest / registry name → directory basename) + normalization. /sibling-sync Guidelines + workflow 3 Mode B step 2 + workflow 4 step 2.2 updated to cite the doc; /synergy-tracker File naming section upgraded from manifest-unaware 'repo slug or directory name' to convention-aligned tier 3/4 derivation. CLAUDE.md Synergy tracking convention bullet rewritten. Backward-compat verified for vp-beads ↔ vp-knowledge. Two skill-reviewer passes returned no blocking issues. npm run check passes (17/17 hook tests + plugin validation + remark + shellcheck/shfmt).
bm-entity example corrected in synergy-entry-format.md (line 167 + naming-rule note after field table); CHANGELOG [0.12.1] Changed bullet added; bundled into v0.12.1 pre-flight

Added workflow 0 (Bootstrap registry) to /vendor-sync: detects vendor/ candidates, derives prefix/branch/package, prompts only for remote and optional local-path, previews + writes both base and .local.json, round-trip validates, and warns if .local.json is not gitignored.

Added workflow 1 step 1b for guided synergy-registry.json + .local.json creation with auto-derivation, two-prompt residual flow, preview-then-approve, round-trip validation, and gitignore check; appended registry-creation offer to workflow 3 step 1.

Converted four hard-stop sites (sibling-sync workflow 1 step 1, sibling-sync error handling, vendor-sync registry-absent block, vendor-sync error handling) to redirects pointing at synergy-tracker workflow 1 step 1b and vendor-sync workflow 0 (Bootstrap registry); synergy-tracker workflow 3 redirect was applied in Wave 1.

Inserted explicit two-name derivation sub-step (sibling + this-project) at start of workflow 1 step 1b. Standardized <this> → <this-project> placeholders throughout SKILL.md. Trimmed redundant parenthetical from bm-entity bullet.

Added workflow 5 (Promote to Basic Memory) mirroring upstream-tracker workflow 6: 6 steps (scan → present → route → write/flag → prune → report) + division-of-labor block. Converted workflow 1 step 8 from inline-write to delegation to workflow 5 to enforce sole-owner boundary. Dropped 'planned'/'future' qualifiers across workflow 4 step 5/8, Sprint Workflow Integration, Guidelines division-of-labor, and design-rationale bullet. npm run check passes for synergy-tracker SKILL.md (only unrelated SWARM-12.md warning, outside scope).

validate-plugin.mjs auditWorkflowReferences function added (~82 lines) — regex /\bworkflow\s+\d+\b(?!\s*\()/gi with position-preserving masking of frontmatter, code fences, headings, and quoted strings. Wave 4 also fixed 21 genuine violations the validator caught (Wave 3 cleanup missed within-skill self-references in vendor-sync, synergy-tracker, and sibling-sync). Validator now clean across all scanned files.

Implemented in skills/sibling-sync/SKILL.md: workflows 2 and 3 each gain a step 6 that delegates to a single AskUserQuestion call (Synergy + Upstream headers, two single-select questions per sibling). New 'Action-menu protocol' section centralizes the dispatch table with plugin-namespaced /vp-beads:synergy-tracker and /vp-beads:upstream-tracker calls plus direct bd create for finding (e). Default read-only contract preserved; --auto-reciprocate continues to bypass menu. Subagent context handled (AskUserQuestion is unavailable per Anthropic SDK; falls back to print-the-command). bd create always uses --type=task to avoid validation-on-create failures from missing required sections. npm run check passes (17/17 tests). Reviews: code-reviewer + skill-reviewer + plugin-validator + claude-code-guide all aligned with plan; 3 review-derived inline fixes applied (timing-clarity at both step-6 call sites, --type=task default, expanded subagent failure-mode note).

Rewrote redirect rule in workflow 1 (Log a new entry) step 1 to distinguish pattern-class observations (→ /synergy-tracker) from artifact-class filable items against sibling-shipped skills/hooks/agents (still upstream-tracker).
Added argument-hint: "[workflow] [package-name]" to upstream-tracker SKILL.md frontmatter, matching the format used by synergy-tracker, backlog-groomer, and vendor-sync.

Added bd graph check --json (schema_version: 1) to retrospective workflow 4 (Check for trend review) under new Health audit subsection — parses clean/cycles/summary, surfaces in RETRO file, conditional on every-4th-sprint detection. Bundled with .9 and .10 in single coherent diff per Wave 1 plan.

Added bd doctor/lint/stale/orphans --json checks to retrospective workflow 4 (Check for trend review) under unified Health audit subsection. Doctor prompts before --fix --yes; lint/stale/orphans require human triage. Documented Claude Hook Completeness false-positive caveat with UPSTREAM-brew--beads.md cross-reference. Orphans falls back to text parsing if --json unavailable. Bundled with .8 and .9.

Added decision-capture path to retrospective workflow 5 (Create beads issues from findings). Recognizes decisions vs tasks/features, prompts user to invoke upstream /beads:decision slash command (NOT vp-beads) or use bd create --type=decision with four-section template (Decision/Rationale/Alternatives Considered/Affects). Decisions stay open while in force; never auto-closed. Bundled with .8 and .10.

Codified two RETRO-10 lessons into swarm-wave skill: (1) added rule 4g 'Single owner per issue' in workflow 1 (Plan a swarm sprint) step 4, citing vp-beads-0e9.6 precedent; (2) added 'Single Owner Per Issue' subsection in references/file-contention-and-clustering.md describing the file-disjointness vs. narrative-coherence trade-off; (3) added a fix-loop iteration cap (>2 halts and escalates) in workflow 3 (Post-wave gate) step 6c. All workflow cross-references use the workflow N (Name) form. npm run check passes.

Added ## Boundaries section to agents/sprint-review.md (read-only contract: never writes files, never invokes Skill tool, defers all mutations to /retrospective, /upstream-tracker, /synergy-tracker, /backlog-groomer). Added 5th example demonstrating canonical end-of-sprint output format (sprint position, commits, beads issues, upstream/synergy status, recommendation). Retained model: inherit per acceptance criteria. npm run check passes (17/17 hook tests, plugin/md/sh all clean).

Extended workflow 2 (Review) with optional inverse-file glob staleness detection (step 5). Reads sibling's SYNERGY-<this-project>.md via the same registry-with-override path-resolution pattern as workflow 3 (Compare with sibling). Surfaces two drift classes: stale aligned rows (e.g., PreCompact retired post-v0.28.0) and missing-this-side rows. Degrades silently if the inverse file is not accessible. Defers deeper bilateral reconciliation to /sibling-sync workflow 2.

Research complete. All four hooks verified to exist in current Claude Code (per official docs). Recommendation: implement PostCompact only (P1, high-value pair to existing PreCompact for sprint-context recovery after compaction); defer SubagentStart+SubagentStop as paired unit pending swarm-wave runtime/research-promotion need; drop TaskCompleted on YAGNI (matcherless, no clear use case once the misframed bd-close trigger is removed). Findings: SWARM-13-research-jqk.md.

Added schema validation for .claude/synergy-registry.json (array, name+file required, file=SYNERGY-{name}.md consistency, npm/ bm-entity warning, known-relationship warning) and .claude/vendor-registry.json (array, prefix+remote+branch+package required as strings, optional local-path string) in validate-plugin.mjs. Skips *.local.json files. npm run check passes.

Resolved 2026-05-08: empirical bd init test in fresh mktemp -d directory confirmed both interactions.jsonl and .beads-credential-key are present in the canonical .beads/.gitignore template generated by bd init. FR entry deleted from UPSTREAM-brew--beads.md per workflow 3 (Resolve).

Over-engineering for current scale per Simplicity-over-Correctness tenet. UPSTREAM/RETRO formats are sufficiently documented via skill prose and references/ files (sprint 12 added several). validate-plugin.mjs provides the audit layer. Machine-readable JSON Schemas would add maintenance burden without proportionate benefit at single-plugin scale. Revival trigger: if 2+ external consumers need to validate against these formats, or if drift between skill docs and actual file structure becomes a recurring bug source.

Triaged 4 stale P4s: deferred emm + 3z0 to 2026-08-01 (still relevant but not urgent), closed dca as over-engineering for current scale (Simplicity tenet), deferred xux to 2026-09-01 (speculative explore-type). P4 backlog dropped from 11 to 7.

Added Dependabot alert summary to hooks/session-start.sh: parses owner/repo from origin remote (SSH and HTTPS forms), queries gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependabot/alerts?state=open with --jq length, emits a single [security] N open Dependabot alert(s) line into additionalContext on positive count. Silent on every failure path (gh missing, no GitHub remote, malformed remote, rate-limited, non-numeric output, count=0, any non-zero gh exit). Single-JSON output contract preserved. Three new tests in scripts/check-hooks.mjs: stubbed gh returning 3 emits security line with repo URL; stubbed gh returning 0 stays silent; gh-missing path emits no security line. Test infra extended with pathPrefix option on runHook plus makeTempGitRepo and makeGhStubDir helpers. npm run check passes (20/20 hook tests, plugin/md/sh all clean).

Resolved by retrospective skill rule binding (commit 1 of reconciliation): file count is canonical for trend-review heuristic; topic-number substitution forbidden. RETRO-12 trend-review backfilled in commit 2. Decision (a) backfill superseded by single trend-review file (no retroactive RETRO splitting).

Resolved: synergy-entry-format.md and synergy-tracker workflow 1 (Log a synergy entry) step 1b now declare the canonical six relationship values; validator's KNOWN_RELATIONSHIPS set is authoritative. Free-form wording removed from docs.
Step 6c clarified: per-wave fix-loop budget covers fix-agent dispatches only; inline orchestrator fixes do not consume budget
Empty-string name guard added to validate-plugin.mjs synergy-registry validation; vp-knowledge/vp-git entries still pass; latent edge case closed
PostCompact hook implemented; check passes; matchers fixture added
Spike findings recorded in SWARM-14-research-0e9.4.md; verdict: defer. bd gate is real but Sprint 10-13 shows in-process Agent+Bash gate works; revisit on CI integration or multi-rig execution.

Spike findings recorded in SWARM-14-research-0e9.5.md; verdict: reject. --wisp-type is a closed enum of agent-runtime events (heartbeat/ping/etc.), not freeform brainstorm capture — markdown files (RETRO-NN.md, SWARM-NN.md) remain the right ephemeral medium.

Applied Option B per Sprint 14 investigation (SWARM-14-rvb-investigation.md): CLAUDE.md issue-types table task row updated to require ## Acceptance Criteria; auto-memory MEMORY.md Core types task line updated to match. This aligns project docs with bd's built-in RequiredSections table and the canonical BM brew/brew-beads source-of-truth. The validation.on-create=error gate (set deliberately in commit e0c8fe7) is preserved unchanged.

Wave 1 spike complete; findings at SPIKE-MIG.1.4-findings.md (sandbox blocked /tmp; wrote to project root instead). Verdict: MIXED (65% charitable / 79% architecturally-adjusted coverage; ~410-485 LOC rework, concentrated in 3 skills). Concurrency-critical claim semantics flagged for MIG.1.3 synthesis.

Wave 1 spike complete. Empirical concurrency test PASSED for swarm-wave's actual patterns (creates up to N=100, edits-to-distinct-tasks up to N=16, subtask race, torn-read, kill-mid-write, full realistic wave). FAIL only on single-task contention (lost-update + multi-field race) — last-write-wins fingerprint, mitigated by swarm-wave's single-owner rule + a ~30 LOC claim-guard wrapper. Findings: /tmp/SPIKE-MIG.1.3-findings.md (+9 evidence logs in /tmp/spike-mig-1.3-logs/). Verdict input for MIG.2: STRONG PASS for swarm-wave use case. Executed via main-thread takeover after sub-agent (Agent B) hit /tmp/+npx sandbox wall.
